    Seyed Mohammad Hejazi Hoseini

    Research Assistant

    York University

    • Email copied
    • CV

    Any Questions?

    Ask my chatbot, it is built around my profile and has access to my latest updates.

    Hi, I’m Seyed Mohammad Hejazi Hoseini, a Research Assistant and Master of Applied Science student in Electrical & Computer Engineering at York University in Toronto.

    My research focuses on AI reproducibility, LLM inference, and software engineering agents. I am particularly interested in improving the reliability, traceability, and repeatability of large language model systems in practical applications.

    My recent work explores reproducibility challenges in modern large language model inference, including evaluation pipelines, retrieval-augmented generation, and agentic workflows, where small sources of nondeterminism can propagate into significantly different outcomes.

    Research Interests

    • AI reproducibility
    • LLM inference
    • Software engineering agents
    • Software engineering for AI
    • AI for software engineering

    Experience

    Research Assistant

    Lassonde School of Engineering, York University

    Jan. 2025 - Present · Toronto, ON

    • Developing a reproducibility framework for inference engines such as llama.cpp and vLLM.
    • Error analysis and cost prediction for software engineering agents.

    Teaching Assistant

    York University

    Jan. 2025 - Present

    Courses: Fundamentals of Data Structures and Design and Analysis of Algorithms; Fundamentals of Data Structures; Advanced Object Oriented Programming; Data Structure for Data Science

    Professors: Jackie Wang; Andriy Pavlovych; Ilir Dema

    Positions: Grader; Invigilator; Lab Demonstrator; Tutorial Leader

    Teaching Assistant

    Amirkabir University of Technology - Tehran Polytechnic

    Feb. 2022 - Jun. 2022

    Courses: Algorithm Design; Data Structures and Algorithms

    Professors: Alireza Bagheri; Sajad Shirali-Shahreza

    Positions: Assessment Designer; Grader
